title: PACU delirium observation documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports periodic documented respiratory, cardiovascular, neurologic or mental, temperature, pain, nausea or vomiting, hydration, urine-output, drainage, and bleeding observations.
assessment.rationale: The exact ASA assessment section supports periodic mental-status or consciousness observation in recovery; it does not diagnose delirium or direct treatment.

source.title: Practice Guidelines for Postanesthetic Care
source.population: Patients recovering from anesthesia or sedation, with clinical and procedural qualifications.
source.exclusions: Exact for periodic recovery assessment domains including respiratory and cardiovascular status, mental status, temperature, pain, nausea or vomiting, hydration, urine output, drainage, and bleeding. Treatment recommendations are not mapped; recency is a blocker.

exact_location.heading: Patient assessment and monitoring during recovery

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports periodic documented respiratory, cardiovascular, neurologic or mental, temperature, pain, nausea or vomiting, hydration, urine-output, drainage, and bleeding observations.

assessment.text: Supports identity, disposition, medical, surgical and anesthetic history, medicines, allergies and reactions, fasting, advance directives, airway and cardiopulmonary examination, tests, consultations, ASA status, plan including postanesthesia and pain care, and consent.
assessment.rationale: The exact ASA preanesthesia documentation section supports risk, airway, cardiorespiratory, reviewed-investigation, consultation, plan, and consent fields for PACU delirium observation documentation; each remains clinician-entered and conditional on assessment.

source.title: Statement on Documentation of Anesthesia Care
source.population: Patients receiving anesthesia services across preanesthesia, intraoperative, and postanesthesia phases.
source.setting: Anesthesia care continuum and anesthesia medical records.
source.exclusions: Exact for documentation fields and continuity of anesthesia records. It supports recording clinician-assessed facts and completed plans, not generating management, medicines, doses, procedures, referrals, discharge, or urgency.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports identity, disposition, medical, surgical and anesthetic history, medicines, allergies and reactions, fasting, advance directives, airway and cardiopulmonary examination, tests, consultations, ASA status, plan including postanesthesia and pain care, and consent.

sources_used.title: Statement on Documentation of Anesthesia Care

additions.final_wording: Supports periodic documented respiratory, cardiovascular, neurologic or mental, temperature, pain, nausea or vomiting, hydration, urine-output, drainage, and bleeding observations.
